MASTER CALENDAR COURT

Plaintiff Chun Yun'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Feb-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Chun Yun'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories (Part 2 of 2) is a duplicate entry. (302)

Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Interoggs
Off Calendar Feb-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ashley Baltazar, Esq. of Sommers Employment Law Group, PC, Tel. 415.524.2860, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopts its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF CHUN YUN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories is GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff alleges that Defendant Joe's Union L.P. misclassified her as exempt and failed to provide rest breaks, meal breaks, and overtime pay during Plaintiff's employment as a pastry chef from January 2012 to September 2012. Plaintiff has moved to compel further responses to Plaintiff's special interrogatories, form interrogatories - General and Employment, Requests for Admissions, and Requests for Production - set one. The court rules as follows: Requests for Admissions: DENIED. Defendant has answered "Deny" to all of Plaintiff's requests for admissions in Plaintiff's Set One RFAs. Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set One. GRANTED. Defendant's objections to Nos. 200.1, 200.4, 207.1, 209.2 and 214.1 are not well taken and the responses are incomplete. Special Interrogatories - General, Set One: GRANTED. Defendant's objections to Nos. 3.2, 3.6, 3.7, 4.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 15.1, and 17.1 are not well taken and the responses are incomplete. In responding, Defendant must state the address and telephone summer of each witness in order to provide sufficient information for Plaintiff to subpoena them for trial. Special Interrogatories - Set One: GRANTED. Defendant's objections to Nos. 4, 6-9, 11-12, 15-16, 18-19, 23, 25-26, 28-34 are not well taken, and the response are incomplete. Request for Production of Documents: GRANTED. Defendant has stated that it will produce only Plaintiff's personnel file and payroll records. They contain information that is partially responsive to some of the requests but Plaintiff's requests seek information relevant to the subject matter in the pending action that is likely to be found in other documents as well. Within 30 days Defendant shall serve Plaintiff with code-compliant verified further responses to Form Interrogatories, Requests for Production, and Special Interrogatories (General and Employment), and produce all responsive documents. Request for sanctions is DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Ernst Halperin; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Interoggs

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Defendant; Request To Deem Plaintiff'S Request For Admission Admiatted; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ashley Ernst, Esq. of Sommers Employment Law Group PC, Tel. 415.432.4372, for plaintiff and moving party;
Alex Santana, Esq. of Ogletree Deakins, Tel. 415.369.3553, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: After hearing, the Court issued the following ruling: PLAINTIFF CHUN YUN's Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Defendant; Request To Deem Plaintiff's Request For Admission Admitted; And For Monetary Sanctions is denied as moot without prejudice to the plaintiff's right to bring a motion to compel additional responses. Request for attorney's fees is denied. Defendant to submit a form of order. Judge Pro Tem: Ernst Halperin; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
